title: Hamas names Khalil al-Hayya as new overall leader
description: The Palestinian armed group's Qatar-based chief negotiator replaces Yahya Sinwar, who was killed by Israeli forces in Gaza in 2024.
content:
The Palestinian armed group Hamas has elected Khalil al-Hayya as its new leader, according to a statement.
He replaces Yahya Sinwar, who was killed by the Israeli military in Gaza in October 2024.
Hayya, who already headed the movement in Gaza from e... [1789 chars]

title: Russian warship carried out firing exercise off Plymouth coast
description: The Neustrashimy's live-fire exercise took place in international waters and lasted for 30 minutes.
content:
A Russian warship, shadowed by a Royal Navy vessel, carried out a live-fire artillery exercise just 40 nautical miles south of Plymouth on Monday.
The Royal Navy monitored the exercise and is continuing to "track the vessel's activity closely", a Min... [1559 chars]

title: Trump imposes 50% tariff on Canadian imports
description: The duties mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.
content:
US President Donald Trump has imposed a 50% tariff on a wide range of goods imported from Canada, in retaliation for what he called "unequal treatment" of US cars, dairy and alcohol.
The duties take effect within 30 days and mark a major escalation i... [3422 chars]

title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
description: The US says the attacks were aimed at degrading Iran's capabilities to attack ships in the Strait of Hormuz.
content:
The US military says it launched another round of strikes against Iran to "further degrade" its capabilities to attack ships in the Strait of Hormuz.
The strikes - the 10th consecutive night of attacks - come after US President Donald Trump vowed to ... [2184 chars]

title: UK's furry bumblebees 'keeling over' in heatwaves
description: The furry bodies of UK bumblebees are specially adapted for cold weather - making heatwaves a struggle.
content:
UK bumblebees could suffer sharp falls in their numbers as heatwaves become more frequent and intense, new analysis suggests.
The findings - shared exclusively with the BBC - draw on data from the Bumblebee Conservation Trust's long-running national ... [4082 chars]
